BAUMGARTNER IN SUPPORT OF STIPULATED EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR SHORTENED TIME ON HEARING AND BRIEFING SCHEDULE FOR MOTION FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF MANDATE (TRANSACTION ID # 62554727) FILED BY RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (SUED HEREIN AS "ANIMAL CARE AND CONTROL," "SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF SUPERVISOR, " SAN FRANCISCO VICIOUS AND DANGEROUS DOG UNIT" AND "JOHN DENNY, HEARING OFFICER, IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY")
PETITIONERS OPENING BRIEF (TRANSACTION ID # 62556659) FILED BY PETITIONER WEOHLER, CHRISTINE THE GUARDIAN TO HER CANINE COMPANION BUSTER

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF MANDATE - THE PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE HEARING OFFICER'S DETERMINATION THAT THE DOG BUSTER IS VICIOUS AND DANGEROUS. (SEE SF HEALTH CODE 42 ET SEQ.) HOWEVER, THE HEALTH CODE REQUIRES CONSIDERATION OF ALTERNATIVE REMEDIES TO KILLING SUCH A DOG. (ID. AT 42.3.) THE REMEDY OF TRANSFERRING OWNERSHIP OF BUSTER TO A PERSON IN A RURAL LOCALE WAS RAISED AT THE ADMINISTRATIVE HEARING, BUT NOT ADDRESSED IN THE HEARING OFFICER'S "STATEMENT OF DECISION." BUSTER'S OWNER SHALL HAVE UNTIL NOVEMBER 2, 2018, TO EFFECT TRANSFER OF BUSTER TO A RURAL LOCALE. A STATUS HEARING IS HEREBY SET FOR NOVEMBER 2, 2018 IN DEPARTMENT 302. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600 =302/RBU

calendar.matters:
Status Hearing
Off Calendar Nov-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christine Kelly, Esq. 415-297-3109 of the Animal Law Office for Petitioner.
Margaret Baumgartner, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3800 of the Office of the City Attorney for Respondent city and County of San Franciso.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. Respondent reports status of "Buster". Petitioner proposes change of ownership to someone in Oregon. Respondent suggests leash, immediate removal from California. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Status Hearing. Off calendar.

Parties to work together on a proposed stipulated order that is verbatim with the parties agreements put forth on the record today. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Motion For Issuance Of Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Oct-18-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Christine Kelly of the Animal Law Office, Tel. No. 1-415-297-3109
appearing for the Petitioner;
Atty. margaret W. Baumgartner, Deputy City Attorney, Tel. No. 1-415-554-3859
appearing for the Respondent.

RULING - A R G U E D; Motion For Issuance Of Writ Of Mandate - The petition for writ of mandate is DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. Substantial evidence supports the hearing officer's determination that the dog Buster is vicious and dangerous. (See SF Health Code 42 et seq.) However, the health code requires consideration of alternative remedies to killing such a dog. (Id. at 42.3.) The remedy of transferring ownership of Buster to a person in a rural locale was raised at the administrative hearing, but not addressed in the hearing officer's "statement of decision." Buster's owner shall have until November 2, 2018, to effect transfer of Buster to a rural locale. A status hearing is hereby set for November 2, 2018 in Department 302. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/rbu
